recaps.text: The Fellowship set out to destroy the One Ring in the fires of Mordor, but on the way the wizard Gandalf fell fighting a demon of fire in the mines of Moria, and the company was later broken: the warrior Boromir was overcome by desire for the ring, and Frodo chose to press on toward the enemy's land. Frodo and his servant Sam slipped away alone to carry the ring into Mordor, while the rest were scattered.

As the Fellowship scattered, orcs seized the hobbits Merry and Pippin, and Boromir died defending them, repenting his lapse at the last. Aragorn, Legolas, and Gimli chase the raiders across Rohan. The captive hobbits escape into an ancient forest and meet Treebeard, eldest of the tree-like Ents. Out on the plains the three hunters encounter Éomer of Rohan, and then are astonished to find Gandalf returned from death - clad now in white and greater in power than before.

Gandalf leads the companions to Rohan and frees its king Théoden from the whispered spells of a traitor, rousing the land to war. At the fortress of Helm's Deep the men of Rohan withstand a night assault by Saruman's army and win the day with Gandalf's timely aid. Meanwhile the roused Ents storm Saruman's stronghold of Isengard and trap the fallen wizard in his tower. Reaching the ruins, the company confronts Saruman, and afterward Pippin looks into a dangerous seeing-stone and is nearly ensnared by the Enemy.

Far to the east, Frodo and Sam are lost in the barren hills until they capture Gollum, the creature who once held the ring, and force him to guide them. He leads them through poisonous marshes to the great black gate of Mordor, which is shut fast. Turning instead to a secret southern path, they are caught by a company of Gondor led by Faramir, Boromir's brother - who, unlike his brother, masters the temptation of the ring and lets them go free.

Gollum leads the hobbits up a long stair toward a hidden pass, secretly plotting betrayal. In a lair of darkness he lures them into the web of the giant spider Shelob, who stings Frodo. Believing his master dead, a grief-stricken Sam takes the ring to finish the quest alone - only to overhear orcs reveal that Frodo is not dead but merely poisoned. The orcs carry Frodo off into their tower, leaving Sam desperate outside its shut gate.
